# Digest: CAT Architectural Standards

**Source:** Aether `skills/cat-architectural-standards/SKILL.md` v1.0.0 (2026-07-11)  
**Extracted:** 2026-07-13 for funding knowledge base  
**Full skill remains authoritative in Aether repo.**

## Three standards

| Tier | Nature | Funding narrative use |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Gold** | Workflow-native, linear gates | Pilot process mapping (farm → sensor → decision → actuate) |
| **Diamond** | Enterprise foundation (security, IaC, HA, observability) | Infra / compliance / production readiness claims |
| **Platinum** | Self-improving AI + data flywheel | Agent R&D, local fine-tune, continuous learning |

**Platinum Edge:** RPi 5 16GB + Hailo-10H field layer; DGX Spark-class hub trajectory; hybrid orchestration.

## HITL (from skill — funding critical)

- Health content, cultural content, **Te Mana Raraunga claims**, or **funding pathways** → HITL + cultural review readiness  
- Diamond infra affecting production/security/sovereignty → HITL  
- Platinum flywheel on real farm/whānau data → HITL  

## Hardware (do not drift in proposals)

- Field: Raspberry Pi 5 16GB + Hailo-10H  
- Do not document Hailo-8 / Hailo-10L / 8GB SKUs as product target  

## How grants-agent uses this

1. Classify funded work primary/secondary tier before drafting.  
2. Align milestones to tier (Gold pilots vs Diamond hardening vs Platinum learning loops).  
3. Never claim a higher tier than evidence supports.
